narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD structural weakness because DXY down 0.5%, bearish news flow from US-Iran diplomacy and oil collapse, and CSM ranks USD at zero against all majors
2. CHF and commodity currency strength because CHF scores 100 on CSM with USDCHF H4 sell confirmed, while NZD and AUD score 80-81 with NZDUSD printing a strong H4 buy signal
3. Gold surge overriding risk-on classification because gold up 3.4% to 4710 signals genuine safe-haven demand running parallel to equities, creating a split between pure risk-on and dollar debasement themes

PRICE CONFIRMS

NZDUSD leads the G10 risk-on move, confirmed by its 0.90 correlation with AUDUSD both pointing higher and a strong H4 buy signal, while USDCHF sell aligns with CHF topping the CSM. CADJPY is the clearest laggard, printing a strong H4 sell with CAD ranked 27 on CSM despite JPY correlating tightly at 0.96 with USDJPY, suggesting yen crosses broadly under pressure from the yen side, not the commodity side. EURJPY and GBPJPY H4 sell signals are consistent with EUR weakness at CSM 35 overwhelming any JPY softness.

REGIME

Mixed, conflicted. VIX at 17.2 declining and S&P500 up 1.4% point risk-on, but gold surging 3.4% simultaneously signals debasement or geopolitical hedging that is inconsistent with a clean risk-on read.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold vs equities: gold up 3.4% and silver up 6.7% while S&P500 rallies suggests dollar debasement is driving both, not a clean risk rotation
2. NZDJPY: NZD is the strongest commodity currency and JPY is being sold in crosses, yet Risk-Off Low regime label persists on D1 and H4 alignment, conflicting with the H4 buy on NZDJPY

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: NZDUSD up - CHF tops CSM but NZD has cleaner USD short expression with strong H4 signal - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: CADJPY down - CAD weakest commodity currency, H4 strong sell confirmed, yen cross momentum aligned - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: AUDUSD - AUD scores 80 CSM but lacks a direct H4 signal and oil collapse complicates commodity bloc coherence
Watch: DXY breaking below 97.50 confirms USD selling acceleration and validates NZDUSD long; a DXY recovery above 98.50 invalidates it

themes.theme: Oil Price Collapse on Peace Deal Hopes
Fed Policy Uncertainty and Productivity Debate
Precious Metals Surge on Lower Yields

key_observation: Dominant theme is US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough crushing the US Dollar across major pairs. Risk-on environment with equity indices at record highs, commodities volatile on energy geopolitical shift, and yield compression pressuring USD safe-haven demand. JPY showing intervention resistance. GBP and precious metals benefiting from USD weakness.
watch: Iran nuclear deal finality and timing, carry trade unwind magnitude, Federal Reserve yield curve guidance, crude oil stabilization levels, equity momentum sustainability, USD/JPY intervention frequency
